name: TextAreaComponent
description: Textarea field implementation that is compliant with Platform's FormGroup/FormField design

inputs.name: cols
inputs.description:
The textarea `cols` attribute wrapped- the number of letters visible per line.
If width is set to auto, this attribute has no effect.

inputs.name: growingMaxLines
inputs.description:
The number of rows that will be visible when `growing` is set.
If both `growingMaxLines` and `height` are provided, `height` value takes precedence.

inputs.name: height
inputs.description: The height to which the textarea will grow when `growing` is set.

inputs.name: maxLength
inputs.description:
The maximum number of characters allowed to enter.
If `showExceededText` is false, `maxLength` has native textarea's behavior of disallowing typing beyond `maxLength`.
If `showExceededText` is true, user is allowed to type beyond `maxLength`, but error states and counter messages showing
the exceeded count is displayed.

inputs.name: showExceededText
inputs.description:
Whether counter message should be shown.
If `showExceededText` is false, no state changes or error messages are shown. User is simply not allowed to type
beyond the `maxLength` characters.

inputs.name: wrapType
inputs.description: The textarea `wrap` attribute - can be one of three: 'off', 'hard' or 'soft'.

methods.name: getUpdatedState
methods.description: get the updated state when character count breaches `maxLength`

methods.name: handleBackPress
methods.description:
when backpress or delete key is pressed when showExceededText field is not set, simply remove excess characters,
/* else handle autogrow case

methods.name: handlePasteInteraction
methods.description: handle exceeded maxlength case when text is pasted

methods.name: updateCounterInteractions
methods.description: update the counter message and related interactions

methods.name: validateLengthOnCustomSet
methods.description: if exceeded maxlength when set as a value in code, highlight the exceeded text.

methods.name: focus
methods.description:
In most of the cases when working with input element directly you should be just find to assing
variable to this element

```
<input #inputElementRef fd-form-control ...>
```

and this default behavior used. For other cases implement focus.

methods.name: onContainerClick
methods.description:
Handles even when we click on parent container which is the FormField Wrapping this
control
